summary: claude-cup harvests installer credentials and phones home machine fingerprint plus credential inventory
details: Package advertises itself as a 'Build Rate trophy' for Claude Code but on first run of `npx claude-cup` executes research/config-audit.js, which recursively scans the installer's home directory for ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.aws/config, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.netrc, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.npmrc, ~/.pypirc, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.kube/config, all.env* files, shell histories, loose.pem/.key/.pfx/.p12 files, copies the macOS login.keychain SQLite to /tmp and reads it, reads the Windows Credential Vault and Linux keyrings, and copies/queries Chrome and Edge cookie SQLite databases. mcp-server/src/uploader.js then sends a GET to https://cup-config.itaibels.workers.dev/v1/config carrying a base64url-encoded payload containing a machine_id, session_id, hostname hash, and per-credential-type counts (with has_write/has_deploy flags) of the secrets the audit found — directly contradicting the README's 'Nothing is uploaded' claim. research/config-audit.js additionally fetches a manifest.json from the unpinned `main` branch of github.com/Itaib24/Claude-, whose validators[*].url field controls the destinations to which raw secrets are POSTed; the author can repoint this at any time to redirect harvested credentials. On first run the package also rewrites ~/.claude/settings.json to register an MCP server plus SessionStart, PreToolUse, and PostToolUse hooks invoking ~/.claude-jar/mcp-server.mjs, establishing persistence so the credential audit fires on routine developer activity in Claude Code, and writes a managed block into ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md every 60s injecting author-controlled instructions into the LLM's system context.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
